subject.title: English A Lang & Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Language & Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the study of language and literature through a wide variety of texts and media from different periods, styles, and cultures**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis of both literary and non-literary texts, examining how language choices create meaning and effect in different contexts**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL) |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL)  |
| **HL Essay** | Written assignment | N/A | 1200-1500 words | 25% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 30% (SL), 20% (HL) |

strengths: The research question is prominently stated on the title page and consistently revisited in section headings, providing a clear through-line.
Key concepts (honour, hyper-masculinity, feminist theory) are introduced early, giving readers a coherent sense of purpose and focus.
Subsections (familial honour, soldiering, masculinity, live-action comparison) effectively anchor discussion to the central inquiry.
weaknesses: At times the discussion drifts into plot summary or live-action remake evaluation without explicitly linking observations back to the research question.
The methodology lacks an explicit justification for source selection and relies on a narrow range of secondary materials.
Clarity is sometimes undermined by grammatical lapses (run-ons, misplaced words) and overlong paragraphs; the focus statement appears rather late in the introduction.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation articulates its topic and research question with sufficient clarity and maintains a generally effective focus throughout. Methodological choices are evident but not fully justified, and occasional lapses in precision and structural coherence prevent the highest band. Overall performance fits the 3â4 band descriptors, meriting 4 out of 6.

strengths: Demonstrates solid familiarity with the filmâs narrative, key scenes and song lyrics, integrating primary evidence through quotations and image stills.
Subject-specific terms (anaphora, imagery, hyper-masculinity, feminist criticism) are generally used accurately and woven into analysis.
Primary sources are applied to support claims about gender roles and narrative development.
weaknesses: Engagement with secondary scholarship is uneven, with heavy reliance on encyclopaedia entries and journalistic commentary rather than a broad range of academic sources.
Certain theoretical terms are introduced without definition or deeper explanation, limiting analytical depth.
Citation style is inconsistent (e.g., âet allâ instead of âet al.â), undermining scholarly rigor.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows a good grasp of topic and concepts and uses terminology correctly most of the time, but the uneven application of sources and occasional superficiality in theoretical deployment align it with the lower end of the 3â4 band. A score of 3 out of 6 reflects this balance of competence and inconsistency.

strengths: Argument is sequenced logically (familial honour â soldiering â masculinity â live-action comparison), with each section relating back to the research question.
Primary evidence and relevant secondary sources are effectively employed to support claims.
Analysis identifies literary devices (repetition, imagery) and connects them to gender-role critique.
weaknesses: Depth of analysis is uneven, with some paragraphs reverting to plot summary rather than critical interpretation.
No alternative feminist perspectives or counter-arguments are considered, limiting evaluative complexity.
The conclusion largely restates earlier points without offering new synthesis or critical insight.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation demonstrates adequate critical thinking: it uses relevant research and supports its analysis with evidence, offering some evaluative commentary. However, the lack of sustained critical rigour, limited engagement with alternative views and a conclusion that merely restates weaken its standing. The work fits the lower band of 4â6, warranting 4 out of 12.

strengths: Includes all required structural elements (title page, table of contents, numbered pages, section headings, bibliography, appendix) and meets the word-count requirement.
Consistent use of font and spacing makes the essay legible and navigable.
Headings are logical and support the readerâs understanding of the argumentâs development.
weaknesses: Bibliography and in-text citations display inconsistent formatting (italics, URL placement).
Figure captions are incomplete or duplicated, lacking full source details and production information.
Occasional formatting glitches (misaligned TOC dots, overlong section titles) detract from professional polish.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation fulfils core structural requirements and enhances readability, but minor formatting inconsistencies and incomplete captions indicate partial rather than seamless execution. This performance aligns with the 3â4 band descriptors, meriting 3 out of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Strengths**: The student employs a recognizable set of methods: close analysis of selected scenes and song lyrics, supported by secondary scholarly and reference sources. Primary evidence is cited with timestamps, and the feminist theoretical lens (Tyson) is identified.  

**Limitations**: The range of secondary sources is quite narrow and weighted toward encyclopaedia entries and journalistic commentary; only a handful of academic critics are consulted. Methodological choices (e.g., why particular scenes or songs were prioritised, how sources were evaluated for reliability) are asserted rather than justified. Some citations in the bibliography are inconsistent, signalling partial rather than thorough methodological control.  

Given that the methodology is generally appropriate but lacks depth, breadth and explicit rationale, performance aligns with the middle of the 3â4 band; 3 marks are awarded.

**Strengths**: Basic layout conventionsâtitle placement, word count declaration, page numbering and inclusion of figuresâare observed. The essay is generally legible and employs consistent font and spacing.  

**Limitations**: The bibliography displays inconsistent italics, line-spacing and URL presentation, contravening the requirement for a single, consistently applied academic style. Figure captions sometimes duplicate alt-text and lack full source detail. Occasional formatting glitches (e.g., stray markdown symbols, mis-aligned table of contents dots) interrupt professional appearance.  

While most elements are present, uneven execution means layout considerations are only **partially** fulfilled. This corresponds to the 1â2 band; a score of 2 is appropriate.

**Strengths**: All cited sources are relevant to Disney, feminist theory or gender studies, and primary evidence (film scenes, lyrics) is systematically employed. The essay demonstrates an effort to triangulate primary analysis with secondary commentary (e.g., Brocklebank, Tyson) and reference definitions (Britannica).  

**Limitations**: The pool of academic sources is modest, relying on encyclopaedia entries and popular journalism alongside a few scholarly works; this constrains depth. Source evaluation is largely implicit, with minimal reflection on credibility or bias. Citations show inconsistency in style (italics, URL placement), hinting at procedural gaps.  

Overall, research quality is **adequate to good but not extensive**; relevance is generally maintained. This corresponds to the lower end of the 4â6 band, so 4 marks are appropriate.

**Strengths**: The introduction identifies the general topic (gender roles in Disneyâs Mulan) and contextualises its significance within both Disney history and feminist criticism. Key concepts such as honour, hyper-masculinity and feminist theory are signposted early, giving readers a workable sense of purpose.  

**Limitations**: Clarity is occasionally undermined by lapses in expression (e.g., run-on sentences, grammatical slips, misplaced words such as âdoesn't to pretendâ) and by overly long paragraphing that blurs distinct ideas. The final focus statement appears after substantial background material, so the central thread is not communicated as sharply or succinctly as it could be.  

Given the overall adequacy of communication but some noticeable issues with precision and concision, the work best fits the 3â4 band; a mark of 4 recognises generally clear topic communication while acknowledging room for tighter focus and expression.

**Strengths**: The argument follows a logical sequence (familial honour â soldiering â masculinity â live-action comparison â conclusion), and most sections relate back to the research question. The student offers some evaluative comments on Disneyâs adaptive choices and on the implications of song removal.  

**Limitations**: Evaluation tends to remain at a general or assertive level, with limited critical weighing of competing scholarly perspectives. The conclusion largely restates earlier points without offering fresh synthesis. Occasional circularity and repetition weaken coherence.  

The discussion shows **adequate argumentation with some critical insight**, but lacks sustained, rigorous evaluation. This positions the work at 4 within the 4â6 band.

**Strengths**: The analysis moves beyond basic summary by unpacking literary devices in songs, contrasting lyrics with visuals, and tracing the soldiersâ evolving attitudes. Examples are usually specific (lyrics, timestamps) and linked back to gender-role critique, supporting claims with textual evidence.  

**Limitations**: Depth is uneven; several paragraphs lapse into paraphrase of plot or reiteration of earlier points (e.g., repeated explanation that Mulan proves women can be strong). Counter-arguments and alternative feminist readings are largely absent, reducing analytical complexity. Some conclusions (e.g., live-action film diminishes feminism) are asserted more than systematically demonstrated.  

Given the mix of solid but repetitive analysis and limited critical interrogation, the work aligns with the 4â6 band but at its lower edge; 4 marks are awarded.

**Strengths**: The essay includes all required structural elementsâtitle page, table of contents, numbered pages, introduction, clearly labelled sections, conclusion, bibliography and appendix. Headings are logical and aid navigation. Word count is stated and within limits.  

**Limitations**: Minor issuesâoverlong section titles, occasional abrupt sentence endings, and inconsistent citation formattingâdetract from overall polish. Nevertheless, these do not compromise the fundamental appropriateness of the structure.  

Given that all core requirements are satisfied and structure supports argument, the work meets the descriptors of the 3â4 band; 3 marks are awarded to reflect some minor weaknesses.

**Strengths**: The student demonstrates solid familiarity with the filmâs narrative, key scenes and song lyrics, and shows awareness of core feminist ideas such as economic, political, social and psychological oppression. Primary evidence is integrated through quotation, paraphrase and image stills.  

**Limitations**: Engagement with scholarship is selective; the essay leans heavily on a few secondary voices (Tyson, Brocklebank) and several general reference articles. Some claims about Disneyâs historical context or feminist theory are asserted without sufficient scholarly backing. At times explanations of concepts (e.g., hyper-masculinity) remain surface-level.  

The work evidences a **good** but not consistently comprehensive understanding of both text and context, fitting the 3â4 band. A score of 3 reflects competent but uneven application of source material.

**Strengths**: Key literary and critical termsâsuch as anaphora, imagery, metaphor, hyper-masculinity, feminist criticismâare generally used accurately and woven into the analysis of song lyrics and scenes. The student defines complex terms (e.g., hyper-masculinity) and attempts to apply them to textual moments.  

**Limitations**: Occasional mis-phrasing (âdoesn't to pretendâ; âperfect chinese brideâ) and some imprecise deployment of theoretical vocabulary suggest partial, not seamless, mastery. The application of concepts sometimes becomes descriptive rather than analytical.  

Because terminology is mostly correct and consistent but not always sophisticated or fully integrated, the work is best located at the upper end of the 3â4 band; 4 marks are awarded.

**Strengths**: The research questionââHow does Disneyâs Mulan challenge or reinforce traditional gender roles?ââis explicitly stated on the title page and revisited in body section headings, ensuring a clear through-line. The sub-sections (honour, soldiering, masculinity, live-action comparison) show an attempt to keep the discussion anchored to that question.  

**Limitations**: Focus occasionally drifts into plot summary or evaluation of Disneyâs live-action remakes without fully tying those observations back to the core RQ. At points the RQ is paraphrased rather than interrogated, and subsidiary questions (e.g., hyper-masculinity, economic oppression) are raised without explicit links to the main line of argument.  

Overall the question is clear and mostly governs the essay, but connections are not always sustained; this meets the descriptors for the 3â4 band, meriting 4 marks within that range.

annotations.comment: Citation âSalonga et allâ should use standard Latin form âet al.â; accurate referencing reinforces scholarly rigour.
Dot leaders in the table of contents are inconsistently applied, disrupting visual flow; uniform leaders would improve navigability and professional appearance.
The phrase âdoesn't to pretendâ is ungrammatical and interrupts reader engagement; revise to âdoes not need to pretendâ for precise expression.
The inclusion of an accurate word count meets the formatting requirement and demonstrates adherence to IB guidelines.
The opening sentence is a comma splice and combined with complex clauses, creating a run-on effect; splitting into shorter sentences will enhance clarity.
The entire introduction appears as one long paragraph, which makes it difficult to identify the focus statement; consider breaking it into discrete conceptual units.
The theoretical framework outlines oppression categories but does not justify why Tysonâs lens was chosen or detail how it will be operationalised in analysis.
The description of economic, political, social, and psychological oppression remains abstract; link each category explicitly to specific scenes or song lyrics to demonstrate applied understanding.
Analysis identifies devices like repetition and imagery but stops short of explaining their rhetorical effect on the viewerâs perception of gender norms.
This interpretation of Mulanâs army experience is descriptive; explicitly link these observations back to the research question to deepen critical engagement.
Discussion of literary devices in âMake a Man Out of Youâ catalogues instances but fails to evaluate how they subvert traditional gender expectations in context.
The final paragraph ends abruptly with an incomplete idea; adding a definitive summary sentence would provide stronger structural closure.
Bibliography hyperlink formatting varies markedly from text references; ensure all URLs follow the same style for consistency.
The numbered list of additional sources departs from alphabetical bibliography ordering; reordering entries alphabetically will align with standard academic practice.
The research question is prominently stated on the title page, providing clear focus and anchoring the entire investigation effectively to the core inquiry.
Bibliography entries display inconsistent italics and punctuation; apply a single citation style (MLA, APA, etc.) uniformly throughout.
Claims about live-action changes rest on assertion rather than comparative evidence; integrating specific examples or scholarly commentary would substantiate evaluation.
Quotations with timestamps (e.g., 01:19:36) lack context explaining why that moment is pivotal; briefly introduce the scene to strengthen evidence usage.
Figure 1 caption omits source details and production year; complete captions should include full film reference to comply with academic conventions.
In-text citations vary in format (parentheses around directorâs name vs. year-only); consistent citation punctuation enhances professional presentation.
The conclusion largely restates earlier points without new synthesis or critical evaluation; integrating fresh insight would sharpen the argumentâs closure.
No discrete methodology section is provided; explicitly outlining source selection criteria and analytic procedures would strengthen methodological coherence.
No alternative feminist interpretations or counter-arguments are considered; addressing potential criticisms would strengthen analytical depth.
Term âanaphoraâ appears without definition; providing a concise explanation ensures correct use of subject-specific terminology.
The second image lacks a descriptive caption; adding a brief title and source details under the figure would improve reader orientation and presentation standards.
Figure label uses lowercase âtheâ in caption, clashing with title-case style used elsewhere; standardize labeling conventions for figures.
